---
title: 'Cache Resources'
description: 'Cache resource classes for managing specific Discord data types'
---

## Overview

Cache resources are specialized classes that manage specific types of Discord data. Each resource extends one of three base classes depending on their relationship to guilds:

- **BaseResource** - Non-guild resources (users, guilds)
- **GuildBasedResource** - Resources that require both guild ID and resource ID (members, voice states)
- **GuildRelatedResource** - Resources associated with guilds but accessed by resource ID (channels, roles, emojis, etc.)

## Resource Types

### Non-Guild Based Resources

These resources exist independently of guilds.

#### Users

Manages Discord user data.

```typescript
class Users extends BaseResource<UserStructure, APIUser> {
  namespace = 'user';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<UserStructure | undefined>;
  raw(id: string): ReturnCache<APIUser | undefined>;
  bulk(ids: string[]): ReturnCache<UserStructure[]>;
  bulkRaw(ids: string[]): ReturnCache<APIUser[]>;
  values(): ReturnCache<UserStructure[]>;
  valuesRaw(): ReturnCache<APIUser[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a transformed user structure
const user = await client.cache.users?.get('123456789');
console.log(user?.username);

// Get raw API data
const rawUser = await client.cache.users?.raw('123456789');

// Get all cached users
const allUsers = await client.cache.users?.values();
```

#### Guilds

Manages Discord guild (server) data.

```typescript
class Guilds extends BaseResource<GuildStructure, APIGuild> {
  namespace = 'guild';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<GuildStructure<'cached'> | undefined>;
  raw(id: string): ReturnCache<APIGuild | undefined>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, id: string, data: GatewayGuildCreateDispatchData): Promise<void>;
  remove(id: string): Promise<void>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a guild
const guild = await client.cache.guilds?.get('987654321');
console.log(guild?.name);

// Remove a guild (also removes all related data)
await client.cache.guilds?.remove('987654321');
```

<Note>
  Removing a guild automatically removes all related resources (channels, roles, members, etc.)
</Note>

### Guild-Based Resources

These resources require both a guild ID and resource ID for access.

#### Members

Manages guild member data.

```typescript
class Members extends GuildBasedResource<GuildMemberStructure, APIGuildMember> {
  namespace = 'member';
  
  get(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<GuildMemberStructure | undefined>;
  raw(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<APIGuildMember | undefined>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, memberId: string, guildId: string, data: APIGuildMember): Promise<void>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<GuildMemberStructure[]>;
  count(guild: string): ReturnCache<number>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a specific member
const member = await client.cache.members?.get('123456789', '987654321');
console.log(member?.nickname);

// Get all members in a guild
const guildMembers = await client.cache.members?.values('987654321');

// Count members in a guild
const memberCount = await client.cache.members?.count('987654321');

// Get members across all guilds
const allMembers = await client.cache.members?.values('*');
```

<Warning>
  Members require the `GuildMembers` privileged intent to be cached.
</Warning>

#### VoiceStates

Manages voice connection states.

```typescript
class VoiceStates extends GuildBasedResource<VoiceStateStructure, APIVoiceState> {
  namespace = 'voice_state';
  
  get(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<VoiceStateStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<VoiceStateStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get user's voice state in a guild
const voiceState = await client.cache.voiceStates?.get('123456789', '987654321');
if (voiceState) {
  console.log(`User is in channel: ${voiceState.channelId}`);
  console.log(`Muted: ${voiceState.mute}`);
  console.log(`Deafened: ${voiceState.deaf}`);
}
```

### Guild-Related Resources

These resources are associated with guilds but are accessed by their resource ID.

#### Channels

Manages channel data.

```typescript
class Channels extends GuildRelatedResource<AllChannels, APIChannel> {
  namespace = 'channel';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<AllChannels | undefined>;
  raw(id: string): ReturnCache<Omit<APIChannel, 'permission_overwrites'> | undefined>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, id: string, guildId: string, data: APIChannel): ReturnCache<void>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<AllChannels[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a channel
const channel = await client.cache.channels?.get('111222333');
if (channel?.isTextGuild()) {
  console.log(`Channel name: ${channel.name}`);
}

// Get all channels in a guild
const guildChannels = await client.cache.channels?.values('987654321');

// Get all cached channels across all guilds
const allChannels = await client.cache.channels?.values('*');
```

#### Roles

Manages guild role data.

```typescript
class Roles extends GuildRelatedResource<RoleStructure, APIRole> {
  namespace = 'role';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<RoleStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<RoleStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a specific role
const role = await client.cache.roles?.get('444555666');
console.log(role?.name);
console.log(role?.permissions);

// Get all roles in a guild
const guildRoles = await client.cache.roles?.values('987654321');
```

#### Emojis

Manages custom guild emoji data.

```typescript
class Emojis extends GuildRelatedResource<EmojiStructure, APIEmoji> {
  namespace = 'emoji';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<EmojiStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<EmojiStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get an emoji
const emoji = await client.cache.emojis?.get('777888999');
console.log(emoji?.name);

// Get all emojis in a guild
const guildEmojis = await client.cache.emojis?.values('987654321');
```

<Note>
  Emojis require the `GuildExpressions` intent.
</Note>

#### Stickers

Manages custom guild sticker data.

```typescript
class Stickers extends GuildRelatedResource<StickerStructure, APISticker> {
  namespace = 'sticker';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<StickerStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<StickerStructure[]>;
}
```

#### Messages

Manages cached message data.

```typescript
class Messages extends GuildRelatedResource<MessageStructure, APIMessage> {
  namespace = 'message';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<MessageStructure | undefined>;
  values(channelId: string): ReturnCache<MessageStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a message
const message = await client.cache.messages?.get('123456789');
console.log(message?.content);

// Get all messages in a channel
const channelMessages = await client.cache.messages?.values('111222333');
```

<Warning>
  Message caching can consume significant memory. Consider using `LimitedMemoryAdapter` with message limits.
</Warning>

#### Presences

Manages user presence data (online status, activities).

```typescript
class Presences extends GuildRelatedResource<PresenceStructure, APIPresence> {
  namespace = 'presence';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<PresenceStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<PresenceStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get a user's presence in a guild
const presence = await client.cache.presences?.get('123456789');
console.log(presence?.status); // 'online', 'idle', 'dnd', 'offline'
console.log(presence?.activities);
```

<Warning>
  Presences require the `GuildPresences` privileged intent.
</Warning>

#### Overwrites

Manages channel permission overwrites.

```typescript
class Overwrites extends GuildRelatedResource<OverwriteStructure[], APIOverwrite[]> {
  namespace = 'overwrite';
  
  get(channelId: string): ReturnCache<OverwriteStructure[] | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<OverwriteStructure[][]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Get overwrites for a channel
const overwrites = await client.cache.overwrites?.get('111222333');
for (const overwrite of overwrites || []) {
  console.log(`Type: ${overwrite.type}`);
  console.log(`ID: ${overwrite.id}`);
  console.log(`Allow: ${overwrite.allow}`);
  console.log(`Deny: ${overwrite.deny}`);
}
```

#### StageInstances

Manages stage channel instances.

```typescript
class StageInstances extends GuildRelatedResource<StageInstanceStructure, APIStageInstance> {
  namespace = 'stage_instance';
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<StageInstanceStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<StageInstanceStructure[]>;
}
```

#### Bans

Manages guild ban data.

```typescript
class Bans extends GuildBasedResource<BanStructure, APIBan> {
  namespace = 'ban';
  
  get(userId: string, guildId: string): ReturnCache<BanStructure | undefined>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<BanStructure[]>;
}
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// Check if a user is banned
const ban = await client.cache.bans?.get('123456789', '987654321');
if (ban) {
  console.log(`Ban reason: ${ban.reason}`);
}

// Get all bans in a guild
const guildBans = await client.cache.bans?.values('987654321');
```

<Note>
  Bans require the `GuildModeration` intent.
</Note>

## Base Resource Classes

### BaseResource

Base class for non-guild resources.

```typescript
class BaseResource<T = any, S = any> {
  namespace: string;
  adapter: Adapter;
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<T | undefined>;
  bulk(ids: string[]): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, id: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  patch(from: CacheFrom, id: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  remove(id: string): ReturnCache<void>;
  keys(): ReturnCache<string[]>;
  values(): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  count(): ReturnCache<number>;
  contains(id: string): ReturnCache<boolean>;
  flush(): ReturnCache<void>;
  hashId(id: string): string;
}
```

### GuildBasedResource

Base class for guild-based resources (requires guild ID + resource ID).

```typescript
class GuildBasedResource<T = any, S = any> {
  namespace: string;
  adapter: Adapter;
  
  get(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<T | undefined>;
  bulk(ids: string[], guild: string): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, id: string, guild: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  patch(from: CacheFrom, id: string, guild: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  remove(id: string | string[], guild: string): ReturnCache<void>;
  keys(guild: string): ReturnCache<string[]>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  count(guild: string): ReturnCache<number>;
  contains(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<boolean>;
  flush(guild?: string): ReturnCache<void>;
  hashId(id: string): string;
  hashGuildId(guild: string, id: string): string;
}
```

### GuildRelatedResource

Base class for guild-related resources (accessed by resource ID, associated with guild).

```typescript
class GuildRelatedResource<T = any, S = any> {
  namespace: string;
  adapter: Adapter;
  
  get(id: string): ReturnCache<T | undefined>;
  bulk(ids: string[]): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  set(from: CacheFrom, id: string, guild: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  patch(from: CacheFrom, id: string, guild: string, data: S): ReturnCache<void>;
  remove(id: string | string[], guild: string): ReturnCache<void>;
  keys(guild: string): ReturnCache<string[]>;
  values(guild: string): ReturnCache<T[]>;
  count(guild: string): ReturnCache<number>;
  contains(id: string, guild: string): ReturnCache<boolean>;
  flush(guild: string): ReturnCache<void>;
  hashId(id: string): string;
}
```

## Common Patterns

### Raw vs Transformed Data

Most resources provide both transformed structures and raw API data:

```typescript
// Transformed structure (recommended)
const user = await client.cache.users?.get('123456789');
// Returns UserStructure with helper methods

// Raw API data
const rawUser = await client.cache.users?.raw('123456789');
// Returns APIUser (plain object)
```

### Bulk Operations

```typescript
// Get multiple users at once
const users = await client.cache.users?.bulk([
  '111111111',
  '222222222',
  '333333333',
]);

// Get multiple members from a guild
const members = await client.cache.members?.bulk(
  ['111111111', '222222222'],
  '987654321'
);
```

### Filtering by Guild

```typescript
// Get resources in a specific guild
const guildChannels = await client.cache.channels?.values('987654321');

// Get resources across ALL guilds
const allChannels = await client.cache.channels?.values('*');

// Count resources in a guild
const channelCount = await client.cache.channels?.count('987654321');
```

### Resource Filtering

Resources have a `filter` method that determines what gets cached:

```typescript
class CustomUsers extends Users {
  // Only cache bot users
  filter(data: APIUser, id: string, from: CacheFrom) {
    return data.bot === true;
  }
}
```

## Usage Examples

### Checking Resource Availability

```typescript
// Resources may be undefined if disabled in cache config
if (client.cache.members) {
  const member = await client.cache.members.get('123', '987');
}

// Or use optional chaining
const member = await client.cache.members?.get('123', '987');
```

### Working with Member Data

```typescript
// Members require both user and member data
const member = await client.cache.members?.get('123456789', '987654321');

if (member) {
  console.log(member.user.username);  // User data
  console.log(member.nickname);        // Member data
  console.log(member.roles);           // Member roles
  console.log(member.joinedAt);        // Join timestamp
}
```

### Iterating Over Resources

```typescript
// Get all guilds
const guilds = await client.cache.guilds?.values();

for (const guild of guilds || []) {
  console.log(`Guild: ${guild.name}`);
  
  // Get channels in this guild
  const channels = await client.cache.channels?.values(guild.id);
  console.log(`  Channels: ${channels?.length || 0}`);
  
  // Get members in this guild
  const memberCount = await client.cache.members?.count(guild.id);
  console.log(`  Members: ${memberCount || 0}`);
}
```

## Best Practices

<Tip>
  **Use Bulk Operations**: When fetching multiple resources, use `bulk()` instead of multiple `get()` calls for better performance.
</Tip>

<Warning>
  **Check Intents**: Ensure you have the required intents enabled for the resources you want to cache.
</Warning>

<Note>
  **Optional Resources**: Always check if a resource is defined before using it, as resources can be disabled in cache configuration.
</Note>

<Tip>
  **Memory Management**: For large bots, consider:
  - Disabling unused resources
  - Using `LimitedMemoryAdapter` with appropriate limits
  - Implementing custom filtering logic
</Tip>
